mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS series lowers the market discount rate and eases financial conditions, so a sustained downtrend in policy rates predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS is falling on a 3-month basis, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening labor income and demand, which should pressure earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 45% of cases, or if the sign of the effect reverses in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in RSAFS and HOUST indicates weakening consumer demand and housing activity, which should reduce near-term revenue expectations and weigh on S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform either series alone in predicting negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal has Rank IC below 0.03 over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is rising while CPIAUCSL is stable or falling, real activity is improving without an inflation shock, which supports higher equity margins and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with rising INDPRO and low CPI inflation do not generate positive 20-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS rate tightens equity valuation multiples by raising discount rates, so policy rates above a restrictive threshold should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in a majority of observations with FEDFUNDS above 4.5%, or if the conditional hit rate is not below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: A continuing gain in PAYEMS without an increase in UNRATE indicates durable labor-market expansion and supporting household income, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises and UNRATE does not rise over the same 3-month window
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor-strength condition fails to produce positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the effect disappears after controlling for recent market trend.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in GDPC1 signals stronger real demand and earnings capacity, which should lift equity prices over a multi-month horizon as slower shocks are absorbed.
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDPC1 growth does not align with positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS weakens while HOUST also deteriorates, both consumption and housing demand are rolling over, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint contraction signal does not yield negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of triggered instances, or if either proxy alone explains the effect equally well.
